commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Mattermost outbound session routing and opaque target resolution so private channel/group-DM ids can produce `group` session keys while keeping `channel:<id>` wire targets, with regression tests.

PR surface: Source +58, Tests +107. Total +165 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main maps Mattermost `P`/`G` channel types to `group` inbound, while the existing outbound route and opaque target resolver still classify non-user/bare 404 targets as `channel`; I did not run a live Mattermost repro.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is contributor real behavior proof plus maintainer selection among multiple open same-issue PRs; no narrow automated repair is identified.

target.title: fix(mattermost): key private channels as group on outbound routing
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Resolves #95646.

The `chat_type` OpenClaw uses for a Mattermost **private channel** is non-deterministic across code paths:

- **Inbound** classifies it by channel *type* â€” `P`/`G` â†’ `group` (authoritative, correct).
- **Outbound / session reconstruction** re-derives it from the delivery *target string* `channel:<id>` (a private channel is addressed with the *same prefix* as a public channel) â†’ `channel`.

So one private-channel conversation is keyed under two session namespaces â€” `agent:<id>:mattermost:group:<id>:thread:<root>` (inbound) and a phantom `â€¦:channel:<id>:thread:<root>` (delivery). Threaded/scheduled deliveries bound to one namespace then fail to match the other (fail-closed delivery, or a conversation split across two session keys).

Root cause in the Mattermost plugin: nothing on the outbound path can represent `group`. `resolveMattermostOutboundSessionRoute` only ever produced `direct` (user) or `channel`, and the opaque target resolver (`resolveMattermostOpaqueTarget`) only classified `user` vs `channel` â€” never `group`.

## Fix

Make the Mattermost outbound path represent `group`, sourcing the authoritative type without a lossy string re-derivation:

1. **`session-route.ts`** â€” `resolveMattermostOutboundSessionRoute` now keys a conversation as `group` when an authoritative signal says so: the resolved target kind is `group`, the target carries an explicit `group:` prefix, or â€” crucially for threaded/scheduled delivery â€” the inbound `currentSessionKey` already records this peer as `group`. The outbound route then shares the inbound `group:<id>` namespace instead of forking `channel:<id>`.
2. **`target-resolution.ts`** â€” `resolveMattermostOpaqueTarget` now classifies a bare channel id by its **real channel type** (`GET /api/v4/channels/<id>`): `P`/`G` â†’ `group`, `O` â†’ `channel`, cached per id. So a direct send addressed by channel id also keys correctly.

The **wire target stays `channel:<id>`** for non-DMs â€” Mattermost posts to the channel id regardless of public/private, and `parseMattermostTarget` only accepts `channel:`/`user:`. The `group` distinction lives in the session key (`peer.kind`), which is exactly the namespace that was mismatching.

## Scope

This is the routing/session half of the Mattermost `group`-overload. The system-prompt-intro half is tracked separately in #95645 (PR #96069). The generic `inferChatTypeFromTarget` ordering is intentionally left unchanged: keying the session at the Mattermost boundary is sufficient and keeps the blast radius to one plugin.

## What changed

- `extensions/mattermost/src/session-route.ts` â€” honor an authoritative `group` signal (resolved kind / `group:` prefix / inbound `currentSessionKey` peer kind) so a private channel keys as `group`.
- `extensions/mattermost/src/session-route.test.ts` â€” new cases: group via resolved kind, group recovered from `currentSessionKey`, and a guard that an unrelated group key does not leak onto a public channel.
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/target-resolution.ts` â€” resolver classifies `P`/`G` channels as `group` via channel-type lookup (cached); wire target stays `channel:<id>`.
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/target-resolution.test.ts` â€” new cases: public `O` â†’ channel, private `P` â†’ group (cached, single lookup), and channel-lookup failure â†’ channel fallback.

## Evidence

- **Source-level repro** (high confidence): inbound `P â†’ group` vs outbound `channel:<id> â†’ channel`; confirmed `resolveMattermostOutboundSessionRoute` only emitted `direct`/`channel` and `resolveMattermostOpaqueTarget` only emitted `user`/`channel`.
- **Regression tests (added)** directly demonstrate the fix â€” the outbound route for a private channel now produces `baseSessionKey: agent:main:mattermost:group:priv123` and recovers the inbound `group:<id>:thread:<root>` session key instead of forking `channel:<id>`; the resolver returns `kind: group` for a type-`P` channel. Public channels and unrelated peers are unaffected.
- Production symptom that motivated this: threaded/scheduled delivery (e.g. a pipeline-watch cron) into a Mattermost private channel fail-closed because the delivery session key (`channel:<id>`) never matched the inbound one (`group:<id>`).
